title: Cephalonoplos ochrolepidium
taxonomy: Cephalonoplos ochrolepidium is a genus of extinct plants belonging to the family Cephalonoplosaceae. This plant is classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The phylum to which Cephalonoplos ochrolepidium belongs is Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants with no seeds or flowers. The kingdom to which Cephalonoplos ochrolepidium belongs is Plantae, the kingdom of all plants. It is also classified under the subkingdom Viridaeplantae.

intro: Cephalonoplos ochrolepidium, also known as Ochrolepis, is a type of fern native to New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cephalonoplos ochrolepidium include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
